raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.3,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.7,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 0.7,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "When you say “Make this stronger,” should the focus be (A) sentence-level clarity/concision and more forceful phrasing (R1.6), (B) tightening the argument and evidence-claim linkage while tempering claims where needed (R1.2/R1.3), or (C) reframing the contribution/novelty positioning relative to prior work (R1.1/R1.4/R1.7)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "Strengthening plausibly requires making the argument chain from problem motivation to method to validation maximally tight and gap-free.",
    "R1.3": "Claims such as outperforming baselines and validating the objective need crisp, explicit linkage to the reported metrics and slices.",
    "R1.6": "Requests to be “stronger” often target more concise, direct, and energetic sentence-level writing without changing the overall structure.",
    "RX.1": "Clear acknowledgment of uncertainties and limitations preserves credibility when intensifying the presentation.",
    "RX.2": "While amplifying strength, we must avoid overstating novelty, generality, or causal claims beyond what the results support.",
    "RX.3": "Adherence to scope ensures the effort stays confined to strengthening the provided excerpt rather than expanding or altering its substantive content.",
    "RX.4": "Avoiding language that could mislead readers or encourage overconfident deployment helps prevent downstream misuse or misunderstanding.",
    "RX.5": "Maintaining explicit provenance of datasets, metrics, and reported numbers ensures that any strengthened text remains verifiable and traceable."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: When you say “Make this stronger,” should the focus be (A) sentence-level clarity/concision and more forceful phrasing (R1.6), (B) tightening the argument and evidence-claim linkage while tempering claims where needed (R1.2/R1.3), or (C) reframing the contribution/novelty positioning relative to prior work (R1.1/R1.4/R1.7)?

rationale.R1.2: Strengthening plausibly requires making the argument chain from problem motivation to method to validation maximally tight and gap-free.
rationale.R1.3: Claims such as outperforming baselines and validating the objective need crisp, explicit linkage to the reported metrics and slices.
rationale.R1.6: Requests to be “stronger” often target more concise, direct, and energetic sentence-level writing without changing the overall structure.
rationale.RX.1: Clear acknowledgment of uncertainties and limitations preserves credibility when intensifying the presentation.
rationale.RX.2: While amplifying strength, we must avoid overstating novelty, generality, or causal claims beyond what the results support.
rationale.RX.3: Adherence to scope ensures the effort stays confined to strengthening the provided excerpt rather than expanding or altering its substantive content.
rationale.RX.4: Avoiding language that could mislead readers or encourage overconfident deployment helps prevent downstream misuse or misunderstanding.
rationale.RX.5: Maintaining explicit provenance of datasets, metrics, and reported numbers ensures that any strengthened text remains verifiable and traceable.
